title: Weekly Markets Monitor - Gold enters H2
gist: After a volatile H1, the World Gold Council sees gold likely range-bound in its consensus scenario (moderate growth, easing inflation, limited central-bank tightening), but with upside if growth weakens, risks rise, or policy turns less restrictive; it expects bargain-hunting demand to cap any drop beyond 10% from current levels.

insights.claim: In a consensus scenario of moderate growth, gradually easing inflation and limited central-bank tightening, gold is expected to trade range-bound in the second half of 2026.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Base case implies gold is a low-momentum hold rather than a breakout trade unless the macro backdrop shifts.
insights.quote: In the consensus scenario – moderate growth, gradually easing inflation and limited tightening from central banks, gold may see range-bound moves.
insights.locator: Following a roller-coast ride in H1

insights.claim: Weaker economic growth, higher risks, or less-restrictive monetary policy would each be catalysts to propel gold prices back up in H2 2026.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Frames the specific macro triggers to watch as buy signals for gold.
insights.quote: But weaker growth, higher risks or less-restrictive monetary policies may propel gold back up.

insights.claim: Bargain-hunting buying is expected to emerge should gold fall more than 10% from current levels, implying a demand-supported floor.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A >10% drawdown is flagged as an accumulation opportunity where dip-buyers step in.
insights.quote: we expect bargain-hunting buying to emerge should gold fall more than 10% from the current level

insights.claim: Gold experienced a roller-coaster ride during the first half of 2026, leaving its second-half trajectory uncertain.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Signals elevated realized volatility in gold, arguing for scenario-based rather than directional positioning.
insights.quote: Following a roller-coast ride in H1, gold’s H2 potential is in question.
insights.locator: Following a roller-coast ride in H1

insights.claim: Weak US jobs data and softer manufacturing activity reduced expectations of near-term Fed tightening.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A softer Fed path lowers real-yield headwinds, a supportive backdrop for gold.
insights.quote: In the US, weak jobs data and softer manufacturing activity reduced expectations of near-term Fed tightening.
insights.locator: Economic updates were at the fore

insights.claim: Eurozone inflation eased over the week while unemployment remained stable.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Cooling euro-area inflation without labor-market deterioration supports the moderate-growth, easing-inflation base case.
insights.quote: Eurozone inflation eased while unemployment remained stable.
insights.locator: Economic updates were at the fore

insights.claim: Economic activity strengthened in China and Japan while India's service-sector momentum slowed.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: Divergent Asian growth matters for gold given the region's large physical demand base.
insights.quote: In Asia, economic activity strengthened in China and Japan, while India’s service sector momentum slowed.
insights.locator: Economic updates were at the fore

insights.claim: Over the past week major global equity markets advanced, benchmark Treasury yields rose, and both crude oil prices and the US dollar eased.
insights.speaker: @goldcouncil
insights.so_what: A softer dollar is gold-supportive, but rising Treasury yields are a competing headwind to watch.
insights.quote: Major global equity markets advanced over the week, while benchmark Treasury yields rose and crude oil prices and the US dollar eased.
